# Unmanageable

> Songs, research, and resources about severe adult ADHD by Brent K. Hubert (Clearly Confused). From inside it, to inside it — not a recovery story, a mirror.

## Pages

- [Home](/): The album "ADHD: Clearly Confused" and the project's thesis.
- [Songs](/songs): All 12 tracks with recommended listening order.
- [About](/about): Who Brent K. Hubert is and why this site exists.
- [Research](/research): Sourced statistics on severe adult ADHD — prison, untreated rates, crashes, suicide, substance use, lifespan.
- [Resources](/resources): Crisis lines, advocacy groups, and evidence-based ADHD education.
- [Community](/community): A reflections space being built in real time.

## Songs

- [Both Things Can Be True](/songs/both-things-can-be-true): The contradiction at the center of ADHD.
- [Brain On Shuffle](/songs/brain-on-shuffle): The anti-"superpower" song.
- [Daydream Terrors](/songs/daydream-terrors): When imagination turns on you.
- [Glitch Cycle](/songs/glitch-cycle): The named loop, still undefeated.
- [I Break Me](/songs/i-break-me): Self-destruction as a performance of worth.
- [Looping Mind](/songs/looping-mind): The room that won't get quiet.
- [Loud Brain, Tired Bones](/songs/loud-brain-tired-bones): The daily operational tax.
- [Shame Keeps Blooming](/songs/shame-keeps-blooming): The gap between insight and execution.
- [The Same Damn Knife](/songs/the-same-damn-knife): Not healed — still here.
- [You Know](/songs/you-know): The torture of knowing.
- [Fast Life (Part 1)](/songs/fast-life-part-1): The engine everyone claps for.
- [Slow Death (Part 2)](/songs/slow-death-part-2): The closer — the part nobody sees.
